nodes.context: Anti-communist rebels who waged guerrilla war against the Sandinista government in Nicaragua from 1979 to 1990.
Marxist political party that came to power after the Nicaraguan Revolution in 1979.
Largest Contra organization, formed in 1981 from merger of several groups, based in Honduras.
Early Contra group formed by ex-guardsmen of the Nicaraguan National Guard.
Early Contra group formed by remnants of the National Guard.
Early Contra group formed by remnants of the National Guard.
Opposition group of Nicaraguan exiles based in Miami, founded by José Francisco Cardenal.
Peasant militia led by disillusioned Sandinista veterans from the northern mountains.
Contra group organized by Edén Pastora in April 1982, embedded in the Democratic Revolutionary Alliance (ARDE).
Contra umbrella group that embedded the Sandinista Revolutionary Front.
Movement among Miskito, Mayangna and Rama Amerindian peoples of Nicaragua's Atlantic coast, in conflict with the government from December 1981.
Breakaway group from Misurasata in 1983, led by Stedman Fagoth Muller, aligned more closely with the FDN.
Umbrella organization of Contra groups formed in June 1985, dissolved early 1987.
Umbrella organization of Contra groups formed in May 1987, similar to UNO.
Contra group listed in infobox as a group within the Contras.
Contra group listed in infobox as a group within the Contras.
Anti-communist rebels who waged guerrilla war against the Sandinista government in Nicaragua from 1979 to 1990.
Marxist political party that took power after the Nicaraguan Revolution in 1979.
Former military force of the Somoza regime; members later formed the Contras.
Secret organization created by Oliver North to support the Contras, with its own airplanes, pilots, airfield, ship, operatives, and Swiss bank accounts.

edges.evidence_quote: The Contras ... were the anti-communist rebels who waged a guerrilla war against the Marxist Sandinista National Liberation Front (FSLN)
edges.context: The Contras waged guerrilla war against the FSLN.

edges.evidence_quote: In September 1981, the CIA and Argentina's intelligence agency ... persuaded 15 September Legion, the UDN, and several former smaller groups to merge as the Nicaraguan Democratic Force
edges.context: The Fifteenth of September Legion merged into the FDN.

edges.evidence_quote: In September 1981, the CIA and Argentina's intelligence agency ... persuaded 15 September Legion, the UDN, and several former smaller groups to merge as the Nicaraguan Democratic Force
edges.context: The UDN merged into the FDN.

edges.evidence_quote: The Misurasata movement split in 1983, with the breakaway Misura group of Stedman Fagoth Muller
the breakaway Misura group of Stedman Fagoth Muller, which aligned more closely with the FDN
edges.context: Misura aligned more closely with the FDN.

edges.evidence_quote: Edén Pastora ... organized the Sandinista Revolutionary Front (FRS) – embedded in the Democratic Revolutionary Alliance (ARDE)
edges.context: The FRS was embedded in ARDE.

edges.evidence_quote: After UNO's dissolution early in 1987, the Nicaraguan Resistance (RN) was organized along similar lines in May.
edges.context: The RN succeeded UNO as the Contra umbrella group.

edges.evidence_quote: By 1987, most of the Contra militias had united into the Nicaraguan Resistance, within which the Nicaraguan Democratic Force was the largest group.
edges.context: The FDN was the largest group within the RN.

edges.evidence_quote: The Contras (from Spanish: contrarrevolucionarios, lit. 'counter-revolutionaries') were the anti-communist rebels who waged a guerrilla war against the Marxist Sandinista National Liberation Front (FSLN) and the Junta of National Reconstruction, which came to power after the Nicaraguan Revolution in 1979.
edges.context: The Contras waged a guerrilla war against the Sandinista government.

edges.evidence_quote: Various groups were created in response to this, consisting of dissidents of the new government and members of the former National Guard.
edges.context: Members of the former National Guard helped form the Contras.

edges.evidence_quote: With the third-party funds, North created an organization called The Enterprise, which served as the secret arm of the NSC staff and had its own airplanes, pilots, airfield, ship, operatives, and secret Swiss bank accounts.
edges.context: The Enterprise was created to secretly fund and supply the Contras.
